title: Ludwigia neograndiflora
taxonomy: Ludwigia neograndiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. This plant is native to Central and South America and is commonly found in aquatic environments such as ponds and lakes. The genus Ludwigia is a group of flowering plants that are typically found in wetland areas and are known for their ability to thrive in a variety of aquatic conditions. The classification of Ludwigia neograndiflora places it within the family Onagraceae, which also includes other aquatic plants such as the Evening Primrose. The specific classification of Ludwigia neograndiflora is further broken down into its kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species levels, providing a detailed understanding of its place within the plant kingdom.
